title: lepidium scapiflorum
intro: Lepidium scapiflorum is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), commonly known as desert cress or mountain peppergrass. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Lepidium scapiflorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which have been reported to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system.
